use clap::Parser;
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

#[derive(Parser)]
struct Cli {
    #[arg(long)]
    output_dir: PathBuf,

    #[arg(long)]
    config: PathBuf,
}

fn validate_path(path: &Path, must_exist: bool) -> Result<PathBuf, String> {
    let canonical = path
        .canonicalize()
        .map_err(|e| format!("cannot resolve {}: {e}", path.display()))?;
    if canonical.starts_with("/proc") || canonical.starts_with("/sys") {
        return Err("refusing to access system directories".into());
    }
    if must_exist && !canonical.exists() {
        return Err(format!("path does not exist: {}", canonical.display()));
    }
    Ok(canonical)
}

fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<(), String> {
    let config_path = validate_path(&cli.config, true)?;
    let output_path = validate_path(&cli.output_dir, false)?;
    std::fs::create_dir_all(&output_path).map_err(|e| format!("mkdir failed: {e}"))?;
    let data = std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path).map_err(|e| format!("read failed: {e}"))?;
    let out = output_path.join("result.txt");
    std::fs::write(&out, data).map_err(|e| format!("write failed: {e}"))?;
    Ok(())
}
